description:
Meeting Transcript Speaker Visualization with Color-Coded Turn Segmentation

Enhance the Transcript tab within the Meeting Details flyout/sidebar panel to visually differentiate speakers using color coding and restructure the raw transcript text into discrete, speaker-attributed turn segments.

Requirements:

Speaker Color Assignment:
- Dynamically assign a distinct color per unique speaker detected in the transcript data
- Use a predefined palette of accessible, high-contrast colors that work in both light and dark mode (leverage existing color system tokens)
- Ensure color assignments are stable within a session (same speaker always gets same color throughout the transcript)
- Support gracefully degrading to a neutral color for unidentified or "Unknown" speakers

Speaker Turn Segmentation:
- Parse the transcript and group consecutive lines/sentences by speaker into discrete turn blocks
- Each turn block must display: speaker name (or label), timestamp or time range of the turn, and the spoken content
- Merge consecutive segments from the same speaker into a single visual block (avoid fragmenting one speaker's turn into multiple cards)
- Preserve chronological order of the full conversation

Visual Design:
- Display a colored left-border or avatar/badge beside each turn block to indicate the speaker
- Show the speaker name as a label above or beside each turn block, styled in their assigned color
- Render timestamps in a muted, secondary style to avoid visual noise
- Ensure turn blocks are clearly separated with adequate whitespace
- Do not alter the existing tab navigation structure (Transcript / Key Points / Action Items tabs remain unchanged)

Speaker Legend:
- Add a compact speaker legend or key at the top of the transcript tab listing each speaker and their assigned color
- Clicking a speaker name in the legend should filter/highlight only that speaker's turns (others dimmed), with a toggle to show all

Edge Cases to Handle:
- Transcripts with a single speaker (no segmentation needed; still apply color)
- Very long speaker turns spanning many paragraphs
- Speaker names that are long strings or contain special characters
- Transcripts where speaker metadata is absent or malformed — fall back gracefully to unsegmented plain text rendering
- Empty transcript state (no change to existing empty state UI)

Data Considerations:
- Audit the existing transcript data shape returned from the Krisp webhook and stored in Neon PostgreSQL to confirm whether speaker attribution is already structured (e.g., array of `{ speaker, timestamp, text }` objects) or needs to be parsed from a raw string
- If parsing is required, implement a robust regex or split strategy that handles common Krisp transcript formats (e.g., "Speaker Name: text" or timestamped blocks)
- No new API endpoints required if transcript data is already fetched for the flyout; extend the existing data pipeline only if speaker fields are missing

Accessibility:
- Do not rely on color alone to distinguish speakers — pair color with speaker name labels and/or distinct avatar initials
- Ensure color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA standards for text on background
title: Add color-coded speaker segmentation to meeting transcripts

summary:
## Summary: Meeting Transcript Speaker Visualization with Color-Coded Turn Segmentation

### Changes Implemented
- **Rewrote transcript parser** to handle Krisp's native inline format (`Speaker Name | MM:SS text`) with pipe+timestamp delimiters, while keeping the fallback colon-separated format (`Speaker Name: text`) for other transcript sources
- **Added timestamp extraction** — each transcript segment now captures the timestamp from the raw content and displays it in a muted mono style next to the speaker name
- **Implemented consecutive speaker merging** — adjacent segments from the same speaker are merged into a single visual block to avoid fragmented turns
- **Made speaker legend interactive** — clicking a speaker name in the legend filters/highlights only that speaker's turns (others dim to 25% opacity), with a toggle to show all. A "Show all" button appears when filtering is active
- **Improved turn block visual design** — increased spacing between blocks (`space-y-2.5`), better padding (`px-3.5 py-2.5`), timestamp displayed inline with speaker name using `flex items-baseline`, smooth opacity transitions
- **Added `useMemo`** for `speakerColorMap`, `segments`, and `allSpeakers` to avoid unnecessary re-parsing
- **Added `allSpeakers` derived collection** that merges speakers from metadata with any additional speakers discovered during transcript parsing
- **Extracted `findSpeakerIndex` helper** for cleaner speaker-to-index matching logic
- **Added `filteredSpeaker` state** that resets when the meeting changes
- **Edge case handling**: graceful fallback for unstructured transcripts, single-speaker transcripts, empty transcripts, and speaker names with special characters (regex escaping preserved)

### Files Modified
- `components/meeting/MeetingDetailDrawer.tsx` — All changes are in this single file

### Notes for Developer
- The parser prioritizes the Krisp pipe+timestamp format (`Name | MM:SS text`). If no pipe-formatted matches are found, it falls back to the colon-separated line-based parser
- Speaker color assignments are stable: determined by the order speakers appear in the meeting's `speakers` metadata array, cycling through the 8-color palette
- The "Show all" button only appears when a speaker filter is active
- Non-filtered speaker blocks dim to 25% opacity with a smooth 200ms transition
- Timestamps render in 10px monospace font next to the speaker name label
- No new dependencies or API endpoints were added
